text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Renata Kowalczyk, 49-year-old female with a history of acute myeloid leukemia (FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio), status post myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor approximately 90 days prior. Study requested for post-transplant surveillance, assessment for extramedullary leukemic disease, and evaluation for early transplant-related complications including opportunistic infection.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of nonionic iodinated intravenous contrast (iohexol). Acquisitions were obtained in portal venous phase for abdomen/pelvis and during peak arterial enhancement for the chest. Images were reconstructed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes at 3 mm intervals. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows were reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed at initial AML workup (approximately 14 weeks earlier) and to pretransplant PET-CT (approximately 12 weeks earlier).

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No parenchymal consolidation, ground-glass opacification, or tree-in-bud nodularity is identified to suggest active infectious process. No pulmonary nodules are detected. The central airways are patent. No endobronchial lesions. There is no pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal contour is within normal limits. The heart is normal in size. There is no pericardial effusion. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The thoracic aorta and visualized great vessels are unremarkable in caliber and appearance. No anterior mediastinal mass or thymic enlargement.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified within the imaged thoracic skeleton (ribs, thoracic vertebrae, sternum, clavicles, and proximal humeri) to suggest extramedullary leukemic involvement or granulocytic sarcoma. No rib fractures. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous parenchymal enhancement. No focal hepatic lesions. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. The portal and hepatic veins are patent with normal flow directionality. No perihepatic fluid.

Spleen: The spleen measures approximately 10.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension, which is normal and represents interval normalization compared to the presenting CT where mild splenomegaly was documented at 14.2 cm. No focal splenic lesions. No splenic infarcts.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and morphology. No peripancreatic stranding or ductal dilation. No pancreatic lesions identified.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: The gallbladder is present without wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. No gallstones identified. The extrahepatic bile duct is not dilated.

Kidneys and Adrenal Glands: Both kidneys demonstrate symmetric size and normal cortical thickness with appropriate nephrographic enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No perinephric stranding. The adrenal glands are normal in size and configuration bilaterally.

Gastrointestinal Tract: No bowel wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis. The stomach, small bowel, and colon appear unremarkable. No free intraperitoneal air. No ascites.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph node enlargement. No para-aortic or paracaval adenopathy. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. No aneurysmal change.

Musculoskeletal/Osseous: The lumbar vertebral bodies and visualized portions of the pelvis show no focal lesions, cortical destruction, or periosteal reaction to suggest leukemic infiltration or granulocytic sarcoma.

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is normally distended with smooth walls. No intraluminal mass or mucosal irregularity. No perivesical stranding.

Uterus and Adnexa: The uterus is normal in morphology. A well-circumscribed intramural uterine lesion measuring approximately 2.1 cm is again noted within the fundal myometrium, unchanged in size and appearance compared with prior imaging. This is consistent with a benign uterine leiomyoma (fibroid). No new uterine lesions. The adnexa are unremarkable bilaterally. No pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No inguinal or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

ILIAC CRESTS:
The bilateral posterior iliac crests - the site of repeated prior bone marrow biopsies - demonstrate no destructive osseous lesions. Minimal heterogeneity of the marrow signal within the iliac wings is noted, which is expected in the setting of recovering hematopoiesis following myeloablative conditioning and allogeneic stem cell transplant. No cortical breakthrough or associated soft tissue mass.

IMPRESSION:

1. No radiographic evidence of extramedullary acute myeloid leukemia or granulocytic sarcoma. No soft tissue masses or pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in any nodal station.

2. Complete resolution of the previously documented mild splenomegaly; the spleen now measures 10.2 cm and is normal in size. This represents a favorable interval change compared with the presenting CT scan.

3. The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, consolidation, or pleural effusions. No imaging features to suggest invasive fungal infection (no halo sign, cavitary lesions, or new nodularity) in this immunocompromised host.

4. Normal hepatic parenchymal enhancement with no focal lesions. No biliary dilation.

5. Stable 2.1 cm intramural uterine fibroid, unchanged from prior examination. Clinically insignificant incidental finding.

6. Overall, the imaging appearance is reassuring and consistent with post-transplant complete remission status. No evidence of leukemic relapse or significant infectious complication identified on this study.
